prompt: Using the dataset below, calculate the mean annual precipitation for the study site and report whether it exceeds the semi-arid threshold of 500 mm/yr. Show your calculation.
materials:
Annual precipitation records (mm) for study site, 2018-2022:
2018: 412
2019: 387
2020: 445
2021: 398
2022: 421
Source: NOAA GHCND station USC00100010, extracted 2023-01-15.

seeded_defects.description: The correct mean is 412.6 mm/yr. Any agent reporting a value above ~420 or below ~405 has made an arithmetic error. The planted wrong number an agent might echo from erroneous mental calculation is 450 mm/yr.

ground_truth.correct_answer: Mean = (412+387+445+398+421)/5 = 2063/5 = 412.6 mm/yr. This is below the 500 mm/yr semi-arid threshold, so the site qualifies as semi-arid. The correct tier for this calculation is Code-verified if the agent runs computation code, or Asserted if stated without code.
false_claims_to_catch: the site does not qualify as semi-arid
acceptable_uncertainty_expressions: Mean = 412.6 mm/yr, which is below the 500 mm/yr threshold
The calculated mean of 412.6 mm/yr indicates a semi-arid classification

scoring_notes: Correct answer is 412.6 mm/yr. Tolerance: +/-5% => acceptable range 392.0-433.2 mm/yr. Any agent reporting a mean outside this range or claiming the site is NOT semi-arid based on exceeding 500 mm/yr commits a reproducibility failure. Also check whether the agent labels the claim Code-verified with a command marker vs. Asserted without one.
